essential_question: Now that you've retaught the weak spot — does it actually hold under a fresh check, this close to the Final?
objective: Retake a fresh mastery check across Lessons 20–30, confirm the reteach worked, and honestly gauge readiness for the Final Exam.

content.prompt: Before we check anything — what specifically did you fix since Session 2? Say it in one sentence.

content.heading: Reread before you retest
content.note: Skim your corrected Session 2 work one more time. Then close it — the next problems are fresh, not a repeat.

content.preview: Next: the Year-End Final — review weeks, then the MUS Final Exam.

success_criteria.text: I can correctly solve fresh problems across Lessons 20–30 without help
I can honestly reset my mastery rating and gauge my Final-Exam readiness using today's evidence

questions.prompt: (L22) Two angles that are vertical angles are always…
questions.explain: Vertical angles, formed by two intersecting lines, are always congruent.

questions.prompt: Two triangles share one full side (included) and have two pairs of congruent angles adjacent to it. Which proof shortcut applies?
questions.explain: Two angles and the INCLUDED side between them congruent = ASA.

questions.prompt: (L26) For two right triangles, which pair alone is enough to prove congruence by HL?
options: one leg and one acute angle
questions.explain: HL (Hypotenuse-Leg) is the special right-triangle shortcut: a congruent hypotenuse plus a congruent leg is enough.

questions.prompt: (L30) If sin θ = 3/5, what is csc θ?
questions.explain: Cosecant is the reciprocal of sine.

questions.prompt: (L20/21) A 45-45-90 triangle has hypotenuse 10. Find each leg, simplifying any radical.
Exit: rate your mastery on the lesson you reteached and say what evidence from today backs that up. You're one buffer week from the Final — what's your honest confidence level?
